description: The figure presents a half-sectional view of a 3-D finite element model simulating the multiaxial forging (MAF) process of Fe-30Mn-9Al-0.8C low-density steel at 250°C using ABAQUS/Explicit. The left subfigure shows the initial configuration before compression, featuring a rectangular deformable workpiece (blue mesh with fine grid) enclosed by rigid die walls (gray mesh). The right subfigure illustrates the deformed geometry after compression, demonstrating significant barrel-shaped expansion in the X-direction (flow direction) and compression in the Y-direction (height direction) while maintaining constraint in the Z-direction. The simulation employs C3D8R hexahedral elements with an optimal mesh size of 0.6 mm (41,382 elements total), reduced integration, and hourglass control. Boundary conditions include a controlled 10 mm displacement of the punch in the Y-direction, fixed die walls, and a friction coefficient of 0.15 at the workpiece-die interface. This visualization validates the finite element setup for investigating plastic flow behavior using a modified Johnson-Cook constitutive model that incorporates evolution of dislocation density and grain size, supporting the simulation of steady-state MAF processes.
